goal: `yaptap` with no flags launches as a persistent macOS menu bar app. A global hotkey (⌥Space by default) starts and stops recording; the result is placed on the clipboard. Prompt selection is sticky via a menu dropdown.

in_scope: Mode detection: no-flags → app mode; any flag → existing CLI pipeline
Non-blocking audio capture API (AudioHandle) shared between CLI and app mode
Shared src/transcription.rs, src/llm.rs, src/audio.rs, src/hotkey.rs, src/config.rs modules
Config file (~/.config/yaptap/config.toml): read/write, hotkey, selected_prompt, whisper_model, llm_model
Single-instance guard via PID lock file
NSApplication in LSUIElement mode (no Dock icon) via cocoa crate
NSRunLoop-based main event loop pumping TrayIconEvent, MenuEvent, and AppEvent channel
Menu bar icon: two-state PNG template images (idle/active), three-bar equalizer design, embedded via include_bytes!()
Tray-icon dropdown menu: prompt list, No Prompt, hotkey display, Open Config, Quit
Sticky prompt selection: persisted to config file on each change
Global hotkey via rdev: configurable key+modifier, modifier-key state tracking via HashSet, Accessibility permission handling via AXIsProcessTrusted
AppState machine: Idle → Recording → Processing → Idle
App-mode recording pipeline: start_recording → stop_and_save → run_transcription → run_llm_collect (buffered) → arboard clipboard
SharedState: Arc<Mutex<Option<AudioHandle>>> + Arc<Mutex<Option<Child>>> for cross-thread cleanup
cleanup_and_exit() shared helper called by Quit menu item and SIGTERM handler
App-mode error handling: alert dialogs via cocoa NSAlert for all pipeline failures
--device <index> CLI flag for explicit audio input device selection
python3 and ffmpeg pre-flight check in app-mode pipeline
panic recovery in pipeline thread via catch_unwind

tasks.title: Add tray-icon, rdev, arboard, dirs, and cocoa to [dependencies] in Cargo.toml
Create src/audio.rs and add `mod audio;` to main.rs: define `AudioHandle` struct holding the live `cpal::Stream`, a shared `Arc<Mutex<Vec<i16>>>` PCM buffer, and the negotiated sample format

Create src/hotkey.rs and add `mod hotkey;` to main.rs: implement `fn parse_hotkey(s: &str) -> anyhow::Result<ParsedHotkey>` where `ParsedHotkey` holds a `Vec<rdev::Key>` of required modifiers and a single main `rdev::Key`; return an error if any token is unrecognised

In src/hotkey.rs: declare `extern "C" { fn AXIsProcessTrusted() -> bool; }` with `#[link(name = "ApplicationServices", kind = "framework")]` so Accessibility permission can be checked without an additional crate

Create src/config.rs and add `mod config;` to main.rs: define `AppConfig` struct with fields hotkey (String), selected_prompt (String), whisper_model (String), llm_model (String); implement Default returning ("option+space", "", "base", "llama3")
Create src/app.rs and add `mod app;` to main.rs: define `pub fn run_app()` as the app-mode entry point

Define `enum AppEvent { HotkeyPressed, PipelineDone(Result<String, String>) }` in src/app.rs; create an `mpsc::channel::<AppEvent>()` in `run_app()` and hold the `Sender` as an `Arc<Sender<AppEvent>>` for cloning into threads

Define `enum AppState { Idle, Recording, Processing }` in src/app.rs; define `struct SharedState { state: Arc<Mutex<AppState>>, audio: Arc<Mutex<Option<AudioHandle>>>, child: Arc<Mutex<Option<Child>>> }`; create a `SharedState` instance in `run_app()`
At the top of main(): if no CLI flags are present (no prompt, prompt-file, list-prompts, model, llm-model, device), call run_app() and return; otherwise fall through to the existing CLI pipeline

Implement `fn start_recording(device_index: Option<usize>) -> anyhow::Result<AudioHandle>` in src/audio.rs: open a cpal input stream targeting 16 kHz mono i16, register the PCM callback to push samples into the shared buffer, and return the AudioHandle immediately without blocking

Implement `AudioHandle::stop_and_save(self) -> anyhow::Result<PathBuf>` in src/audio.rs: drop the cpal Stream to stop capture, encode the PCM buffer to a named temp WAV file using hound, return the path

Create src/transcription.rs and add `mod transcription;` to main.rs: implement `fn run_transcription(wav_path: &Path, model: &str, active_child: &Arc<Mutex<Option<Child>>>) -> anyhow::Result<String>` — store the spawned `Child` in `active_child` before waiting, clear it on completion; update CLI path in main() to call this function

Create src/llm.rs and add `mod llm;` to main.rs: implement `fn run_llm_collect(transcript: &str, prompt_path: &Path, model: &str, active_child: &Arc<Mutex<Option<Child>>>) -> anyhow::Result<String>` — store the spawned `Child` in `active_child` before buffering stdout to a String; keep the existing token-streaming inline in main() for CLI mode

Implement `fn show_alert(title: &str, message: &str, buttons: &[&str]) -> usize` in src/app.rs using `cocoa::appkit::NSAlert`: create an NSAlert, set the message text, add one button per entry in `buttons`, run modally, and return the index of the clicked button
Implement config file path helper in src/config.rs: `fn config_path() -> PathBuf` returning `~/.config/yaptap/config.toml` via `dirs::home_dir()`

Move `fn prompts_dir() -> Option<PathBuf>` from main.rs into src/config.rs as a `pub fn`; update main.rs and src/app.rs to call `config::prompts_dir()` so both CLI and app mode share the same binary-relative resolution logic

In AppConfig::load(): create `~/.config/yaptap/` directory via `std::fs::create_dir_all()` if absent; serialize `AppConfig::default()` to TOML using the `toml` crate and write it to config.toml if the file does not exist
In AppConfig::load(): read config.toml and deserialize via `toml::from_str::<AppConfig>()`; return the parsed struct on success (error cases handled by P3-T017)
In AppConfig::load(): on TOML deserialisation error call `show_alert()` with message "Config file is not valid TOML: ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml — using defaults." and return AppConfig::default()

In AppConfig::load(): call `parse_hotkey()` on the hotkey field; on parse failure call `show_alert()` with message "Unknown hotkey '<value>' — using default: option+space." and reset the field to "option+space" in the returned struct
In AppConfig::load(): if selected_prompt is non-empty and no matching `<stem>.toml` exists in `config/prompts/`, silently reset it to "" in the in-memory struct (do not rewrite the file)

Implement `AppConfig::save_prompt(stem: &str)`: write the full config to `config.toml.tmp` then rename to `config.toml`; on EXDEV (cross-device rename) fall back to copy-then-delete; on any other failure log a warning to stderr and continue

Add `--device <index: usize>` optional CLI flag to the existing Args struct; pass the value as `Some(index)` to `start_recording()` in CLI mode; this flag alone (with no other flags) still triggers CLI mode per P3-T002

Update CLI mode in main(): replace the former blocking capture call with start_recording(args.device), block on stdin Enter detection via `read_line()`, then call handle.stop_and_save() before continuing to transcription

Implement single-instance guard in `run_app()`: if `~/.config/yaptap/yaptap.lock` exists, read the stored PID and call `kill(pid, 0)`; if alive exit 0; if dead (ESRCH) or absent, write own PID as a decimal string to the lock file
In run_app(): call `AppConfig::load()` and store the result; call the single-instance guard (P3-T021) which may exit early
In run_app(): set the NSApplication activation policy to `NSApplicationActivationPolicyProhibited` via the cocoa crate (`NSApp().setActivationPolicy_()`) so no Dock icon or app menu bar appears
Create assets/icons/ directory

Create assets/icons/yaptap-idle.png (18×18px @1×): three filled black rectangles 3px wide with 2px gaps, heights left=6px centre=10px right=6px, centred in the bounding box, transparent background (macOS template image format)
Create assets/icons/yaptap-idle@2x.png (36×36px @2×): same idle design at double resolution for Retina displays
Create assets/icons/yaptap-active.png (18×18px @1×): three filled black rectangles 3px wide with 2px gaps, all bars 10px tall, centred, transparent background
Create assets/icons/yaptap-active@2x.png (36×36px @2×): same active design at double resolution for Retina displays

In src/app.rs: embed all four icon PNGs at compile time via `include_bytes!()` and expose as constants `ICON_IDLE_1X`, `ICON_IDLE_2X`, `ICON_ACTIVE_1X`, `ICON_ACTIVE_2X`; use these byte slices everywhere icons are loaded (eliminates runtime filesystem dependency)

In run_app(): create a `TrayIcon` from the embedded idle icon bytes (P3-T033) via `tray_icon::Icon::from_rgba()`; mark it as a macOS template image so the system renders it correctly in light and dark menu bar themes
Implement `fn switch_icon(tray: &TrayIcon, state: &AppState)` in src/app.rs: load idle bytes when Idle, active bytes when Recording or Processing, and call `tray.set_icon()`

Implement `fn load_prompts(dir: &Path) -> Vec<PromptEntry>` (where `PromptEntry` holds stem, name, description): read all `.toml` files from the directory sorted alphabetically by stem; skip files that cannot be parsed without error

Implement `fn build_menu(prompts: &[PromptEntry], config: &AppConfig) -> (Menu, MenuIds)` where `MenuIds` maps each `MenuId` to a semantic role (`PromptStem(String)`, `NoPrompt`, `OpenConfig`, `Quit`); construct the tray-icon `Menu` from prompt entries
In build_menu(): if `prompts` is empty, add a single disabled "No prompts found" item in place of the prompt list
In build_menu(): add a checkmark to the `MenuItem` whose stem matches `config.selected_prompt`; add the checkmark to "No Prompt" when `selected_prompt` is ""
In build_menu(): add a separator after the prompt list, then a "No Prompt" item, then a second separator
In build_menu(): add a disabled hotkey display item formatting the configured hotkey string with Unicode modifier symbols (option→⌥, cmd→⌘, ctrl→⌃, shift→⇧, space→Space), e.g. "Hotkey: ⌥Space"
In build_menu(): add an "Open Config…" item, a separator, and a "Quit YapTap" item; register their `MenuId`s in the returned `MenuIds`

On each `TrayIconEvent::Click` (icon clicked): call `load_prompts()` and `build_menu()` to rebuild the menu from disk and set it on the TrayIcon before the OS displays it, so newly added or removed prompt files are reflected without restarting

Implement `fn cleanup_and_exit(shared: &SharedState)` in src/app.rs: lock `shared.child` and call `.kill()` if Some; lock `shared.audio`, take the handle, and call `stop_and_save()` discarding the result; delete `~/.config/yaptap/yaptap.lock`; call `std::process::exit(0)`
In the event loop `MenuEvent` handler: look up the clicked `MenuId` in `MenuIds`; dispatch to the appropriate handler (prompt selection, No Prompt, Open Config, or Quit)
On "Open Config…" `MenuEvent`: spawn `open ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml` as a subprocess
On "Quit YapTap" `MenuEvent`: call `cleanup_and_exit()` (P3-T067)

On prompt item `MenuEvent`: update in-memory `config.selected_prompt` to the clicked stem; call `AppConfig::save_prompt(stem)`; call `load_prompts()` + `build_menu()` and set the new menu on the TrayIcon
On "No Prompt" `MenuEvent`: set `selected_prompt` to ""; call `AppConfig::save_prompt("")`; rebuild and set the updated menu

In run_app(): call `AXIsProcessTrusted()` (P3-T010); if it returns false, call `show_alert()` with message "YapTap needs Accessibility access to capture the global hotkey. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility?" and buttons ["Open Settings", "Later"]; on "Open Settings" (button index 0) spawn `open "x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_Accessibility"`; skip hotkey registration

If Accessibility permission is granted: spawn a background thread that calls `rdev::listen()` passing a callback; pass a clone of the `AppEvent` Sender into the callback closure; handle the `rdev::listen()` error return by sending the error back over the channel (P3-T051)

In the rdev callback: maintain a `HashSet<rdev::Key>` updated on `EventType::KeyPress` (insert) and `EventType::KeyRelease` (remove); on each `KeyPress` event, check whether the pressed-keys set contains all required modifiers and the main key from `ParsedHotkey`; only then send `AppEvent::HotkeyPressed` over the Sender

If `rdev::listen()` returns an error: call `show_alert()` with message "The hotkey '<configured-value>' could not be registered. Edit ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml to choose a different hotkey, then restart YapTap."

Register an app-mode SIGTERM handler via the ctrlc crate (termination feature): in app mode, call `cleanup_and_exit()`; ensure this does not overwrite the existing CLI-mode SIGINT handler (which exits 130) — gate the handler on whether run_app() was entered

On audio capture error in app mode (cpal stream error callback fires): send `AppEvent::PipelineDone(Err("audio device error: <message>"))` so the main loop transitions to Idle and shows an alert; delete temp WAV if present
In the main event loop: on `AppEvent::HotkeyPressed`, read the current state; if Idle, call the start-recording handler; if Recording, call the stop-and-pipeline handler; if Processing, do nothing
Start-recording handler: call `start_recording(None)` from src/audio.rs; store the returned `AudioHandle` in `shared.audio`; transition `AppState` to Recording; call `switch_icon(Active)`
At the start of the pipeline thread: check that `python3` and `ffmpeg` are available on PATH; if either is absent, send `AppEvent::PipelineDone(Err("python3 not found"))` or similar and return
In the pipeline thread: take the `AudioHandle` from `shared.audio` (lock + take); call `handle.stop_and_save()` to obtain the temp WAV path; propagate errors via `PipelineDone(Err(...))`
In the pipeline thread: call `run_transcription(wav_path, &config.whisper_model, &shared.child)` to obtain the transcript; propagate errors via `PipelineDone(Err(...))`

In the pipeline thread: if `config.selected_prompt` is non-empty, resolve `<binary_dir>/config/prompts/<stem>.toml`; call `run_llm_collect(transcript, prompt_path, &config.llm_model, &shared.child)`; propagate errors via `PipelineDone(Err(...))`
In the pipeline thread: if `config.selected_prompt` is empty, use the raw transcript string as the output; send `AppEvent::PipelineDone(Ok(output))` on success
In the pipeline thread: delete the temp WAV file (if it exists) unconditionally after the pipeline completes — whether it succeeded, errored, or was abandoned

Wrap the entire pipeline thread body in `std::panic::catch_unwind`; on panic, send `AppEvent::PipelineDone(Err("internal error: pipeline panicked"))` so the state machine always transitions back to Idle
Stop-and-pipeline handler: transition `AppState` to Processing (icon stays Active); clone `shared` and the `AppEvent` Sender; spawn a pipeline background thread passing both
In the main event loop: on `AppEvent::PipelineDone(Ok(output))`, write output to clipboard (P3-T063); transition to Idle; call `switch_icon(Idle)`
In the main event loop on `PipelineDone(Ok(output))`: write the output string to the system clipboard via `arboard::Clipboard::new()?.set_text(output)`; propagate clipboard errors as an alert
In the main event loop: on `AppEvent::PipelineDone(Err(msg))`, transition to Idle; call `switch_icon(Idle)`; call `show_alert()` with the error message

Implement the main event loop in `run_app()`: loop calling `NSRunLoop::currentRunLoop().runUntilDate_(NSDate::dateWithTimeIntervalSinceNow_(0.016))` via cocoa to pump OS events; after each iteration, drain `TrayIconEvent::receiver()`, `MenuEvent::receiver()`, and `app_event_rx` via `try_recv()` and dispatch to handler functions
Verify `cargo build` succeeds with Phase 3 dependencies and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes with no warnings
Verify `yaptap` with no args shows the menu bar icon in idle state (varying bar heights) and does not appear in the Dock
Verify the menu prompt list matches config/prompts/ sorted alphabetically and the checkmark reflects the persisted selected_prompt after restart

Verify pressing the hotkey once switches the icon to active state; pressing again runs the full pipeline and the clipboard contains the LLM output (or raw transcript if No Prompt); icon returns to idle
Verify `yaptap --prompt email-reply` still records, transcribes, and streams LLM output to stdout (Phase 2 CLI regression check)
